text: AVX2 improves CPU inference by processing multiple vector elements per instruction, reducing scalar overhead in matvec and projection kernels. OpenMP improves throughput by distributing decode and prefill work across cores while preserving locality when affinity is configured carefully.

NUMA locality matters because memory traffic that crosses sockets or distant cache domains increases latency and reduces effective bandwidth. A disciplined inference stack pins worker threads, stabilizes page placement, and measures thread migrations as a first-class regression signal.

A benchmark is only useful when the run contract is reproducible. Record the model digest, git commit, native library build identifier, thread tuple, environment variables, and kernel-level timing artifacts so hotspot movement can be trusted.
